children.heading: .—State Defined
children.label: a .—State Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “State” means a State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and Puerto Rico.

children.label: A
children.text: develop transportation plans and programs;

children.label: B
children.text: plan, engineer, design, and evaluate a public transportation project; and

children.label: C
children.text: conduct technical studies relating to public transportation.

children.label: A
children.text: Studies related to management, planning, operations, capital requirements, and economic feasibility.

children.label: B
children.text: Evaluating previously financed projects.

children.label: C
children.text: Peer reviews and exchanges of technical data, information, assistance, and related activities in support of planning and environmental analyses among metropolitan planning organizations and other transportation planners.

children.label: D
children.text: Other similar and related activities preliminary to and in preparation for constructing, acquiring, or improving the operation of facilities and equipment.

children.heading: .—Purpose
children.label: c .—Purpose
children.text: To the extent practicable, the Secretary shall ensure that amounts appropriated or made available under section 5338 to carry out this section and sections 5303, 5304, and 5306 are used to support balanced and comprehensive transportation planning that considers the relationships among land use and all transportation modes, without regard to the programmatic source of the planning amounts.

children.label: i
children.text: the population of urbanized areas in each State, as shown by the latest available decennial census of population; bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the total population of urbanized areas in all States, as shown by that census.

children.heading: .—Minimum apportionment
children.label: B .—Minimum apportionment
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), a State may not receive less than 0.5 percent of the amount apportioned under this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: considers population of urbanized areas;

children.label: B
children.text: provides an appropriate distribution for urbanized areas to carry out the cooperative processes described in this section;

children.label: C
children.text: the State develops in cooperation with the metropolitan planning organizations; and

children.label: D
children.text: the Secretary approves.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall apportion 20 percent of the amounts made available under subsection (g)(1) among the States to supplement allocations made under paragraph (1) for metropolitan planning organizations.

children.heading: .—Formula
children.label: B .—Formula
children.text: The Secretary shall apportion amounts referred to in subparagraph (A) under a formula that reflects the additional cost of carrying out planning, programming, and project selection responsibilities under sections 5303 and 5306 in certain urbanized areas.

children.heading: State Planning and Research Program.—
children.label: e State Planning and Research Program.—
i
children.text: the population of urbanized areas in each State, as shown by the latest available decennial census; bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the population of urbanized areas in all States, as shown by that census.

children.heading: .—Minimum apportionment
children.label: B .—Minimum apportionment
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), a State may not receive less than 0.5 percent of the amount apportioned under this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Supplemental amounts
children.label: 2 .—Supplemental amounts
children.text: A State, as the State considers appropriate, may authorize part of the amount made available under this subsection to be used to supplement amounts made available under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: not to require a State or local match; or

children.label: B
children.text: to allow a Government share greater than 80 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: the applicable urbanized area;

children.label: ii
children.text: the applicable rural area;

children.label: iii
children.text: an adjoining urbanized area; or

children.label: iv
children.text: an adjoining rural area.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: B .—Report
children.text: A State or metropolitan planning organization that carries out an activity described in subparagraph (A) with an increased Government share described in that subparagraph shall report to the Secretary, in a form as determined by the Secretary, how the increased Government share for transportation planning activities benefits commuting and other essential travel in parts of the applicable urbanized area or rural area described in subparagraph (A) with lower population density or lower average income levels.
82.72 percent shall be available for the metropolitan planning program under subsection (d); and
17.28 percent shall be available to carry out subsection (e).

children.heading: .—Availability of Funds
children.label: h .—Availability of Funds
children.text: Funds apportioned under this section to a State that have not been obligated in the 3-year period beginning after the last day of the fiscal year for which the funds are authorized shall be reapportioned among the States.
